summary: Backend + Frontend testing of VSM Ambassador PWA. 10/10 pytest tests pass against the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Login → dashboard → all sub-pages (withdraw, leaderboard, resources, notifications, settings) → logout flow verified on desktop. Mobile drawer + m-logout-btn working at 375x812. PWA manifest, sw.js and icons all return 200. Found 1 real backend bug: /api/ambassador/apply does NOT de-duplicate applications for an existing user and the /me endpoint returns the LATEST application, so re-applying for an already-approved user can flip the dashboard back to 'pending'. This bug was reproduced live (test_user briefly showed CANDIDATURE EN COURS D'EXAMEN); state was cleaned up after testing.

critical.issue: No idempotency / dedup on ambassador_applications. Calling /apply with an email that already has an application inserts a new row with status='pending'. Combined with /api/ambassador/me returning the LATEST application by created_at desc, this can overwrite an approved user's status and push them back to the Pending screen. Reproduced live during testing.

minor.issue: ApplyResponse.already_existed is computed only from the Auth signup step; it is still false even when an ambassador_applications row already exists for that user_id. Consider checking applications table first and returning the existing application_id.

ui_bugs.issue: On invalid login, the error toast/JSON-parse path logs 'Failed to execute json on Response: body stream already read' in console. UI still recovers and shows error to user, but the error handling code reads the response body twice.

data-testid is placed on a wrapper/label such that Playwright's locator.check() times out (element not recognized as checkbox). Standard click works in real users, but for stable automated testing, attach data-testid directly to the underlying <input type=checkbox> element.

action_items: Fix duplicate ambassador_applications insertion in POST /api/ambassador/apply: before inserting, check for existing application by user_id and either return the existing one or update it (or restrict /me to non-rejected/latest-approved logic).
Move data-testid on consent checkboxes (accept-truth/terms/contact) onto the actual <input type=checkbox> so automated tests can drive them via .check().
Fix LoginForm error path that double-reads the fetch Response body (the console error 'body stream already read').
critical_code_review_comments: server.py /api/ambassador/apply has no dedup against existing applications; combined with /me's order=created_at.desc&limit=1, this can demote an approved ambassador back to pending. RCA confirmed during this run.
server.py uses os.environ[...] at import time for SUPABASE_* keys — good (fails fast). Keep that pattern.
verify_user_token calls Supabase /auth/v1/user on every request to /me; consider caching by JWT for short windows if traffic grows.
Frontend manifest/sw/icons all return 200 — PWA basics are correct.
Dashboard exposes good data-testid coverage on every stat/share/CTA — excellent for testability.

success_rate.frontend: ~95% — every required data-testid present on dashboard, withdraw, resources, settings, mobile drawer, logout. Only apply form submit could not be driven end-to-end by Playwright because of consent-checkbox testid placement (not a functional bug).

seed_data_creation: Pytest created 1 new ambassador per run (test-<epoch>@example.com). The session-scope cleanup fixture in backend_test.py automatically removes any extra 'pending' applications inserted for the seeded test user so the approved state is preserved for subsequent frontend testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live in /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py and produce JUnit XML at /app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml. The seeded approved ambassador is application_id=24, status=approved — if you ever see the dashboard show 'Candidature en cours d'examen' for yann.test-1780920203, it means a stray pending row exists; delete pending rows for user_id 78dcfb4a-32af-4b29-8a85-dace5e585b63 via the Supabase service key (DELETE /rest/v1/ambassador_applications?user_id=eq.<uid>&status=eq.pending). The auto-cleanup fixture handles this when pytest runs to completion, but a crashed test run can leave residue.
rca of the issue: RCA — Dashboard flipping to Pending: Step 1 — /api/ambassador/apply receives a payload whose email already has an Auth user. Step 2 — supabase_signup returns None (already exists); supabase_get_user_by_email returns the existing user_id. Step 3 — supabase_insert_application inserts a brand-new row with status='pending' (no uniqueness check on user_id). Step 4 — /api/ambassador/me queries ambassador_applications with order=created_at.desc&limit=1, so the new pending row is returned instead of the approved one. Step 5 — RequireAuth/RouteGuard in App.js routes status=pending users to the Pending page. Reproduction: POST /api/ambassador/apply twice for an approved user → login → user lands on Pending instead of Dashboard. Mitigation (recommended): before inserting, SELECT existing application by user_id; if found, return existing application_id with already_existed=true (no insert). Alternative: change /me to prefer status='approved' application over latest.
